w:r.w:t: 本单元教学以话题讨论为主要形式，引导学生围绕网络安全主题展开深入研讨。最终，学生需在老师带领下制作一条时长三至五分钟的网络安全主题短片，在实践中深化对主题的理解。
Identify common cyber security risks in everyday digital life (e.g. unsafe links, sharing personal information) using basic Chinese.
Ask and answer questions in Chinese about safe and unsafe online behaviours.
Compare their own online habits with others and discuss safe choices in a simple and age-appropriate way.
Students with prior learning and experience
Use a wider range of vocabulary and sentence structures to explain cyber security concepts, such as online safety rules and responsible digital behaviour.
Describe and justify safe online practices using sequencing words and cause-and-effect language.
Write a short reflective text or scenario-based response in Chinese about how to stay safe online.
Students who are learning Chinese as a second or additional language
Use key vocabulary and sentence patterns to identify basic cyber security ideas.
Respond to simple questions about online activities using sentence frames and visual support.
Participate in guided role-plays to practise safe online behaviours.
ML2-INT-01, ML2-UND-01, ML2-CRT-01, ML3-INT-01, ML3-UND-01, ML3-CRT-01
Based on “Be an eSafe Kid”, students will be guided to think, reflect, and discuss online safety issues.
什么是密码？为什么不能告诉别人？
为什么不能用生日、1234 当密码？
为什么要先想一想再点链接？
什么情况会让你觉得“不安全”？
如果你觉得怪怪的，可以怎么做？
在网上，可以做哪些事情来保护自己？
网上看到的东西一定是真的吗？
奇怪的消息或奖励是真的吗？
当看到网上的流行行为时，可以不思考就跟着效仿吗？
Be Kind（文明、友善的网络交流）
网上可以骂人吗？为什么？
看到别人被欺负可以怎么做？
The following vocabulary and grammar may be incorporated.
网络、上网、手机、平板、电脑、游戏、视频、信息、病毒
朋友、同学、老师、家人、陌生人
名字、年龄、学校、地址、电话、邮箱、照片、密码、隐私
告诉、分享、点击、相信、保护、问
可以……: 上网时遇到不确定的信息，可以问老师。
不可以……：不可以分享密码。
应该……：上网时应该保护隐私。
不应该……: 不应该相信陌生人。
Show students how to use their arms to make a circle “
” for 可以 / 安全 and a cross “

” for 不可以 / 危险, then say several online behaviours in Chinese, such as 分享密码, 问老师, 点击信息, and 告诉陌生人名字, and have students respond with the circle or cross gestures to show whether they think each action is safe or unsafe, then briefly discuss their responses

Mini Scenario: The class is divided into small groups, and each group draws a scenario card, such as "Someone asks for your name" or "Receiving a link from a stranger." Students discuss the scenario and use the vocabulary and sentence patterns they have learned to express what they would do. Groups then share their responses with the class.

Memory Game: The class is divided into small groups. Each group is given a set of vocabulary cards with the English word on one card and the corresponding Chinese on another. All cards are placed face down. Students take turns flipping two cards at a time to find a matching pair. If they find a match, they keep the pair and get another turn. The group with the most pairs at the end wins.

Sentence Building Race: The class is divided into small groups. Each group draws a scenario card, such as "Someone asks for your name" or "Receiving a link from a stranger." Students discuss the scenario and quickly create sentences using the vocabulary and sentence patterns they have learned.
Would you share your password with anyone other than your family? Why or why not?
If someone asks you to share your account, what would you do?
What information should you never share online? (e.g., address, school, phone number)
When you register a game account online, what information do you fill in? Which information can you leave blank?
What would you do if you receive a message from a stranger?
If a website asks you to download something, what should you do first?
What would you do if someone says something rude to you online?
What would you do if you see someone being bullied online?
Can you trust new friends you meet online? Why or why not?
What do you think a safe social network looks like?
If you encounter something unsafe online, who would you ask for help?
Have you ever seen a website or message that felt strange or scary? What did you do?

Give students a short scenario and ask them to write 2–4 Chinese sentences describing what they would do. (Example: You receive a link from a stranger. / Someone is being mean to you in a game. / A friend asks for your password.)
Compare commonly used apps and online rules in China and Australia.
Compare common slangs or abbreviation used online in China and Australia.
《数码素养系列》（广东话）（共七条短片）
《新媒體素養提升計劃》（共六条短片）
2022青少年科普影片製作比賽 初中組 LS-V148-Cyb 網絡安全
秘笈09 網絡欺凌零容忍 (旁白版本)
國際互聯網安全日2018 -「智慧網樂遊」短片創作比賽「小學組」冠軍
健康地使用互聯網及電子屏幕產品
【团团微课：青少年网络素养公开课】小学生第1课：互联网伴我成长
【团团微课：青少年网络素养公开课】小学生第2课：防范网络有害信息
【团团微课：青少年网络素养公开课】小学生第3课：上网不可太沉迷
【团团微课：青少年网络素养公开课】小学生第4课：上网要把隐私护
小学生网络安全教育微课视频
《数码素养系列》（普通话）（共七条短片）
2024反詐騙素養宣導｜國小篇｜網路交友詐騙
2024反詐騙素養宣導｜國小篇｜線上遊戲詐騙
與孩子安全共遊網路世界-辨識網路謠言 [7-12歲]
电子书｜《智 Smart 新世代》（小學篇）
电子书｜《点点与你畅游网络世界》
电子书｜《魔法毛与魔法喵：守护网络安全的勇者》
: Create an eSafety poster or rule list in Chinese and present it to the class.
: Role-play a conversation about an online safety situation.
: Write a short dairy in Chinese about a day with the proper usage of technology.
and when they occur in the
and skills as well as the
content focus of each unit of
What would you delete and why:
